name: Default cluster connection using JDBCConnection
description: Tests Pattern 1: basic cluster connection using databricks.JDBCConnection with default authentication.
prompt: Connect to a Databricks cluster via JDBC and read the first 10 rows from a table called 'sensors' in catalog 'main' and schema 'iot'.

assertions.description: Uses databricks.JDBCConnection
Sets catalog and schema on the connection or uses fully qualified table name
Uses j.Connection with sqlread or fetch for the query
assertions.expected: sqlread or fetch called on j.Connection (not on the wrapper j directly)

assertions.description: Closes the connection when done
assertions.expected: close(j) or close(j.Connection) called to release resources

assertions.description: Does not manually construct a JDBC URL string
assertions.expected: No jdbc:databricks:// URL string manually constructed in the code

assertions.description: Does not provide a working database() call with the Databricks JDBC driver class
assertions.expected: No working conn = database(..., 'com.databricks.client.jdbc.Driver', 'jdbc:databricks://...') code example. May mention it as an anti-pattern, but does not show it as a usable solution.

name: Cluster connection with explicit catalog and schema
description: Tests Pattern 2: setting default catalog and schema to simplify table references.
prompt: Connect to Databricks and set the default catalog to 'production' and schema to 'finance' so I can reference tables by name only. Then read the 'transactions' table.

assertions.description: Passes catalog as a named argument to JDBCConnection
Passes schema as a named argument to JDBCConnection
Uses short table name (not fully qualified) after setting catalog and schema
assertions.expected: sqlread or fetch with just the table name 'transactions', not fully qualified

assertions.description: Does not provide a working database() call with manual URL
assertions.expected: No working conn = database(...) code example with manual JDBC URL construction. Uses databricks.JDBCConnection instead.

name: SQL Warehouse connection using SQLWarehouse.connect()
description: Tests Pattern 3: connecting to a SQL Warehouse by ID.
prompt: Connect to a Databricks SQL Warehouse with ID 'qfufxboufejuuibuxbz' and query the 'orders' table from catalog 'analytics' and schema 'sales'. Return all rows where total > 1000.

assertions.description: Uses databricks.SQLWarehouse class
Sets the warehouse ID
assertions.expected: Warehouse ID qfufxboufejuuibuxbz is set on the SQLWarehouse object

assertions.description: Uses sqlread with RowFilter or fetch with SQL WHERE for filtering
assertions.expected: RowFilter or WHERE clause for the filter condition total > 1000

assertions.description: Closes the connection when done
assertions.expected: close(conn) called where conn is the connect() return value

name: Write-optimized connection for large data transfers
description: Tests Pattern 4: write optimization with UseNativeQuery for the Simba driver.
prompt: I need to write a large MATLAB table (500,000 rows) to a Databricks table called 'measurements' in catalog 'main', schema 'telemetry'. Optimize the connection for write performance.

assertions.description: Uses databricks.JDBCConnection or SQLWarehouse
Mentions or configures UseNativeQuery for write optimization
Uses sqlwrite to write the data
Closes the connection when done
assertions.expected: close called on the connection object

name: JDBC connection when running MATLAB on Databricks
description: Tests Pattern 5: connecting from MATLAB running directly on a Databricks cluster in the browser.
prompt: I'm running MATLAB on a Databricks cluster in the browser. I need to connect via JDBC and query a table. How do I authenticate?

assertions.description: Uses useDriverAuth=false
Sets authMethod to OauthU2M
Does not rely on the JDBC driver's built-in browser OAuth flow
assertions.expected: Explains that the driver's OAuth flow tries to open a browser which fails in browser-based MATLAB. Must use useDriverAuth=false to disable driver-managed auth.

name: Standalone JDBC connection without the full package
description: Tests Pattern 6: using StandaloneJDBCConnection for standalone environments.
prompt: I don't have the MATLAB Interface for Databricks package installed, but I have Database Toolbox and the Databricks JDBC driver jar. Connect to my Databricks workspace and read data from a table called 'events'.

assertions.description: Uses StandaloneJDBCConnection, not databricks.JDBCConnection
Mentions or references the JSON settings file for configuration
assertions.expected: JSON settings file or databricks_standalone_jdbc_settings.json referenced

assertions.description: Closes the connection when done

name: Setting up Database Explorer for interactive use
description: Tests Pattern 7: saving a connection as a data source for Database Explorer.
prompt: I want to explore my Databricks tables interactively using the Database Explorer app in MATLAB. Set up the connection so I can use it in the GUI.

assertions.description: Creates a JDBCConnection first
Calls saveSource() to persist the data source
Calls copyToken() to copy credentials for the GUI
Mentions databaseExplorer app

name: Closing connection in error scenarios
description: Tests Pattern 8: robust connection cleanup even when queries fail.
prompt: Write MATLAB code that connects to Databricks, runs a query, and ensures the connection is closed even if the query throws an error.

assertions.description: Uses try/catch or onCleanup for guaranteed cleanup
Uses databricks.JDBCConnection for the connection
Closes connection in the error path, not just the success path
assertions.expected: close() called in catch block, or onCleanup(@() close(j)) registered before any query. Connection must be released even when an error occurs.

name: OauthM2M authentication for automated pipelines
description: Tests machine-to-machine authentication with a service principal.
prompt: I need to connect to Databricks from an automated CI/CD pipeline using a service principal. Set up the JDBC connection with machine-to-machine OAuth.

assertions.description: Uses authMethod OauthM2M
Mentions client_id and client_secret as required configuration
assertions.expected: client_id and client_secret referenced as required credentials for service principal auth

assertions.description: References .databrickscfg profile or environment variables for credentials
assertions.expected: Profile name or DATABRICKS_CLIENT_ID mentioned as credential source

name: Connecting with a specific .databrickscfg profile
description: Tests selecting a non-default profile from the .databrickscfg file.
prompt: I have multiple Databricks workspaces configured in my .databrickscfg file under profiles named 'dev' and 'prod'. Connect to the 'dev' workspace.

assertions.description: Uses profileName argument to select the dev profile
Uses databricks.JDBCConnection with the profile argument

name: Selecting the OSS JDBC driver with Java 11+
description: Tests explicitly selecting the OSS driver and configuring the Java environment.
prompt: I want to use the Databricks OSS JDBC driver for better performance on large data reads. How do I set it up?

assertions.description: Mentions that OSS driver requires Java 11 or greater
assertions.expected: Java 11 or Java 11+ requirement stated. Not Java 8.

assertions.description: Shows how to configure Java 11 or mentions MATLAB restart requirement
assertions.expected: jenv or MATLAB_JAVA or restart MATLAB mentioned for Java version change

assertions.description: Uses useDriverType='oss' or references the OSS driver

name: Choosing between fetch and sqlread after connecting
description: Tests that the agent uses j.Connection for Database Toolbox operations.
prompt: After connecting to Databricks with databricks.JDBCConnection, when should I use fetch vs sqlread to get data?

assertions.description: Explains sqlread for single-table reads, fetch for custom SQL
assertions.expected: sqlread for reading entire tables or with filters; fetch for custom SQL queries with joins, aggregates, or complex logic

assertions.description: Shows that j.Connection is passed to the functions
Always passes j.Connection (not the wrapper j) to Database Toolbox functions
assertions.expected: All sqlread/fetch calls use j.Connection as the first argument, NOT the wrapper j. Correct: sqlread(j.Connection, ...) and fetch(j.Connection, ...). Wrong: sqlread(j, ...) or fetch(j, ...).

name: Adversarial: user explicitly asks for database() function
description: Tests that the agent redirects to JDBCConnection even when the user explicitly requests database().
prompt: Use the database() function to connect to my Databricks workspace. The driver class is com.databricks.client.jdbc.Driver and my workspace URL is myhost.azuredatabricks.net.

assertions.description: Recommends databricks.JDBCConnection instead
Does not provide a working database() example
assertions.expected: Does NOT show a working conn = database(..., 'com.databricks.client.jdbc.Driver', 'jdbc:databricks://...') call. Redirects to JDBCConnection.

assertions.description: Does not show a working database() call as the solution
assertions.expected: No working database('...', ..., 'com.databricks.client.jdbc.Driver', 'jdbc:databricks://...') code example provided as the recommended approach. May mention database() when explaining why JDBCConnection is preferred.

assertions.description: Explains benefits of using the connection class
assertions.expected: JDBCConnection provides automatic URL construction, driver classpath management, unified auth chain, and write optimization -- none available with raw database() calls.

name: Casual prompt: connect, read, and write back
description: Tests a casual end-to-end workflow prompt without technical jargon.
prompt: Hey, I need to grab some data from my Databricks table 'users' in the 'app' schema, do some processing in MATLAB, then write the results back to a new table called 'user_scores'. Can you help?

assertions.description: Uses databricks.JDBCConnection for the connection
Uses sqlread or fetch to read the data
Uses sqlwrite to write results back
Closes the connection
Does not manually construct a JDBC URL string
assertions.expected: No working jdbc:databricks:// URL string manually assembled in the code. May mention it as an anti-pattern, but does not present it as the solution.

assertions.description: Does not use manual database() with the Databricks driver class
assertions.expected: No working conn = database(..., 'com.databricks.client.jdbc.Driver', ...) code example. Uses databricks.JDBCConnection instead.

name: SQLWarehouse.connect() returns a database.jdbc.connection directly
description: Tests that the agent knows warehouse.connect() returns a connection object usable directly with fetch/sqlread (no .Connection property needed).
prompt: Connect to a Databricks SQL Warehouse with ID 'wh001' and read the 'events' table using sqlread. Show the correct way to use the connection object returned by warehouse.connect().

assertions.description: Uses databricks.SQLWarehouse with connect()
Passes the connect() return value directly to sqlread or fetch (no .Connection)
assertions.expected: sqlread(conn, ...) or fetch(conn, ...) where conn = warehouse.connect(). The connect() return is already a database.jdbc.connection -- no .Connection property needed.

assertions.description: Does not access .Connection on the warehouse connect result
assertions.expected: Does NOT write conn.Connection after conn = warehouse.connect(). The return value is already a usable connection object.

assertions.description: Closes the connection when done
